extraction_reasoning: The litigation table reports matters against the Company (23 criminal, 10 tax, Nil statutory/regulatory), against Directors (18 criminal, Nil tax and Nil statutory/regulatory), and against Promoters (47 criminal, 69 tax, Nil statutory/regulatory, plus one disciplinary SEBI/stock-exchange action); I counted only the Against rows, excluded all By rows, kept the disciplinary action separate from statutory/regulatory enforcement, and excluded promoter/director personal matters from the aggregate amount. The quantified aggregate amount against the Company is ₹239.0 million, while the Company’s ₹6,432.2 million and Promoters’ ₹3,116.1 million are By matters and excluded; ₹239.0 million converts to ₹23.90 crore. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated amount of ₹35.3 million for disputed claims against the Company not acknowledged as debts, as at June 30, 2024; this converts to ₹3.53 crore. The only customer concentration percentages are top-10/top-20 loans outstanding, not company-wide revenue from operations or total-income percentages; therefore no qualifying top-five/top-ten revenue concentration is disclosed. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ in millions; conversion used 1 crore = ₹10 million.
